LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS KAMALJIT SINGH, UNION CAB COMPANY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE ENTRY OF DEFAULT - DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO VACATE DEFAULT IS GRANTED. MR. LIBARLE'S DECLARATION DEMONSTRATES ATTORNEY MISTAKE AND RELIEF IS MANDATORY UNDER CCP 473. THE COURT AWARDS $1,500 TO PLAINTIFF FOR THE TIME SPENT ATTEMPTING TO PROCURE THE DEFAULT AND JUDGMENT. MOVING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: REYES HUNTER, CSR #6576, EMAIL:RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG. (302)

Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default; Declaration Of Marc L. Libarle, Esq.; Declaration Of Kamaljit Singh
Off Calendar Oct-25-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT KAMALJIT SINGH, UNION CAB COMPANY'S Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default

Defendants Singh and Union Cab's motion to vacate default is GRANTED. Mr. Libarle's declaration demonstrates attorney mistake and relief is mandatory under CCP 473. The court awards $1,500 to plaintiff for the time spent attempting to procure the default and judgment.

Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, email:rhunter@sftc.org, Reported. (D302)

Hearing On Whether A Bond Should Be Required
Off Calendar Mar-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Raymond M. Alexander, Jr., of Law Offices of Raymond M. Alexander, Jr., Tel. 415.989.9011, for plaintiffs;
Marc Libarle, Attorney at Law, Tel. 415.928.2400, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. Hearing On Whether A Bond Should Be Required is taken off calendar pursuant to plaintiff's agreement to waive objection and post the bond. Defendants shall prepare the proposed order. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Feb-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Raymond M. Alexander, Jr., of Law Offices of Raymond M. Alexander, Jr., Tel. 415.989.9011, for plaintiffs;
Marc Libarle, Attorney at Law, Tel. 415.928.2400, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The parties have met and conferred. The parties stipulate to waive the bond today, and return to discuss the bond at a later hearing date. Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction is taken OFF CALENDAR. The Court continues the action to March 7, 2017, at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302, for hearing on whether a bond should be required. No pleadings need to be filed in advance. The parties may file pleadings if they wish, on or before March 2, 2017. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Jan-11-2017 Continued To Feb-07-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction is CONTINUED to February 7, 2017 at 9:30 AM in Departmetn 302. The declaration of Marc Libarle establishes good cause for the continuance. Supplemental opposition papers shall be filed and served no later than January 25, 2017. Any reply papers shall be filed and served no later than February 1, 2017. The temporary restraining order issued on December 19, 2016 shall remain in effect until the ruling on the order to show cause regarding preliminary injunction. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk
